ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV (Noberus) ransomware execution: --access-token CLI flag and safe-mode boot tampering
ucs.description: Hunts the ALPHV/BlackCat Rust ransomware binary's distinctive command-line invocation (--access-token, --paths, --child, --no-prop, --propagated) chained with the actor's signature pre-encryption defense evasion: bcdedit safeboot reconfiguration plus VSS deletion. These exact CLI argument names are unique to the Noberus payload and have been documented by Microsoft (DEV-0237/Storm-1175 affiliates), CrowdStrike, and CISA AA22-152A.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*--access-token*" AND (Processes.process="*--paths*" OR Processes.process="*--child*" OR Processes.process="*--no-prop*" OR Processes.process="*--propagated*")) OR (Processes.process="*bcdedit*" AND Processes.process="*safeboot*" AND Processes.process="*network*") OR (Processes.process="*vssadmin*" AND Processes.process="*delete shadows*" AND Processes.process="*/all*" AND Processes.process="*/quiet*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | stats values(*) as * count min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where count >= 1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let alphv_args = pack_array("--access-token", "--child", "--no-prop", "--propagated", "--paths");
let payload = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ProcessCommandLine has "--access-token"
  and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("--paths", "--child", "--no-prop", "--propagated")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, SHA256;
let safeboot = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
  and ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot" and ProcessCommandLine has "network"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let vss = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" or FileName =~ "wmic.exe")
  and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadow", "delete")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine;
union payload, safeboot, vss
| summarize Signals = make_set(strcat(FileName, " :: ", ProcessCommandLine)), DistinctSignalTypes = dcount(FileName), MinTime = min(Timestamp), MaxTime = max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName
| where DistinctSignalTypes >= 1
| order by MaxTime desc
ucs.rationale: The --access-token / --child / --propagated argument scheme is a fingerprint of the ALPHV Rust binary (the affiliate must paste a token issued by the BlackCat panel to detonate). No legitimate admin tool or other ransomware family uses that argument set, so a single hit is essentially confirmed BlackCat. The bcdedit safeboot network reconfiguration is BlackCat's documented trick to reboot into saf

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV affiliate pre-encryption staging: ADFind + LSASS dump + remote-access tool deploy
ucs.description: Hunts the pre-encryption tradecraft chain repeatedly attributed to BlackCat affiliates (Scattered Spider/UNC3944 and DEV-0504 among them): domain enumeration with ADFind/ADRecon, LSASS credential dumping, then deployment of legitimate remote-access software (AnyDesk, Splashtop, ConnectWise/ScreenConnect) on the same host within 72 hours. Per CISA AA23-353A and Microsoft, this exact sequence preceded BlackCat detonations against healthcare, MGM Resorts, and Caesars.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as process_names from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("adfind.exe","AdFind.exe") OR Processes.process="*Invoke-ADRecon*" OR (Processes.process="*-f*" AND Processes.process="*objectcategory=*" AND Processes.process="*-csv*") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") OR Processes.process_name IN ("AnyDesk.exe","Splashtop.exe","SRService.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientSetup.exe","atera_agent.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval signal=case(match(process_name,"(?i)adfind|adrecon"),"discovery", match(cmdlines,"(?i)comsvcs\.dll.*MiniDump|procdump"),"lsass_dump", match(process_name,"(?i)anydesk|splashtop|screenconnect|connectwise|atera|srservice"),"remote_access") | stats dc(signal) as stages values(signal) as stages_seen values(process_name) as procs min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where stages >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let window = 3d;
let adfind = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("adfind.exe","AdFind.exe")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("objectcategory=", "-f")
   or ProcessCommandLine has "Invoke-ADRecon"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, Stage="discovery", Evidence=ProcessCommandLine;
let lsass = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
     or (ProcessCommandLine has "lsass" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-ma","dump","MiniDump"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, Stage="lsass_dump", Evidence=ProcessCommandLine;
let rmm = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("AnyDesk.exe","Splashtop.exe","SRService.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientSetup.exe","atera_agent.exe","AteraAgent.exe")
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AnyDesk.exe","Splashtop.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, Stage="remote_access", Evidence=FileName;
union adfind, lsass, rmm
| summarize StageSet = make_set(Stage), StageCount = dcount(Stage), Earliest = min(Timestamp), Latest = max(Timestamp), Evidence = make_set(Evidence, 25) by DeviceId, DeviceName
| where StageCount >= 2 and (Latest - Earliest) <= window
| order by Latest desc
ucs.rationale: Any one of these three signals is noisy on its own (admins legitimately use AnyDesk; ADFind is sometimes used by IT). The high-fidelity twist for BlackCat affiliates — particularly Scattered Spider, the cluster behind MGM/Caesars — is the *combination* on a single host within ~72h: domain enumeration via ADFind, LSASS dumping (often via comsvcs.dll MiniDump or ProcDump renamed), then dropping a le
